# Research Gap Map

## Gap 1: commercial manipulation is under-theorized

Political disinformation and influence operations are more developed research areas than commercial manipulation. Yet the same coordination logic now appears in creator marketing, reviews, app stores, launch platforms, ads, streaming, and startup metrics.

## Gap 2: video-first manipulation is harder to study

Text-based detection is easier than multimodal detection. Short-form video campaigns involve clips, captions, hashtags, audio, comments, repost timing, visual reuse, and account histories.

## Gap 3: hidden labor infrastructure is under-measured

Clippers, editors, VAs, creator farms, synthetic-media operators, agencies, and outsourced moderation can make “organic” success appear individual and spontaneous.

## Gap 4: downstream harm is under-integrated

There are separate literatures on fake reviews, ad fraud, dark patterns, recommender systems, and startup finance. Fewer studies model how signal distortion changes sales, fundraising, cultural visibility, or trust over time.

## Gap 5: legitimate optimization vs deception is unstable

Many tactics live in gray zones. The cleanest distinction is whether the campaign improves truthful discoverability or fabricates the signals of demand.
